skill.name: internal-comms-community
skill.description: Helps draft internal communications such as 3P updates, newsletters, FAQ responses, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.
skill.summary: Draft internal communications in structured company formats.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported network, external command, and weak cryptography patterns. Review found these are false positives from Apache License reference URLs, ordinary license text, and Markdown code spans naming example files. No malicious intent, prompt injection, command execution, data exfiltration, or risky runtime behavior was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: License Reference URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are Apache License reference links in LICENSE.txt. They are static legal text and do not make network requests or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs appear inside the standard Apache License text. No executable code or instruction asks the model to access them.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Apache License Text Matched Weak Crypto Patterns
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings in LICENSE.txt are substring matches inside ordinary Apache License legal language. No cryptographic API, algorithm selection, or security implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are legal prose from the Apache License, not code. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak crypto use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown inline code spans around example file paths. They document which guideline files to load and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown formatting for paths such as examples/company-newsletter.md. There is no Ruby code, shell command, or user input execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Skill Prose Matched Weak Crypto Patterns
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md weak cryptography findings are natural language text in front matter and usage guidance. No cryptographic operation or unsafe algorithm is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are descriptive communication guidance. The file contains no code, imports, crypto APIs, or algorithm names.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear updates that match familiar internal formats. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code draft structured updates, newsletters, FAQs, and reports.
actual_capabilities: Selects a communication type from the user request.
Supports 3P updates for progress, plans, and problems.
Drafts company newsletters and company-wide updates.
Creates FAQ responses for common internal questions.
Drafts status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.
Asks for clarification when the requested format is unclear.
limitations: Does not include the referenced examples directory in this package.
Cannot verify company facts without user-provided context.
Does not send messages or publish communications.
Does not enforce legal, HR, or compliance approval.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Weekly Team Update
use_cases.description: Turn notes into a concise 3P update that separates progress, plans, and problems.

use_cases.title: Draft a Company Newsletter
use_cases.description: Create an internal newsletter from announcements, milestones, reminders, and upcoming events.

use_cases.title: Write an Incident Report
use_cases.description: Convert incident details into a clear internal report with impact, status, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Status Update
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have short notes and need a structured team update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a 3P update from these notes. Separate progress, plans, and problems. Keep it concise and clear for an internal team audience: [paste notes].

prompt_templates.title: Newsletter Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when preparing a recurring company-wide message.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a company newsletter using these announcements, milestones, reminders, and dates. Use a professional internal tone: [paste details].

prompt_templates.title: Leadership Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when executives need a compact update with decisions and risks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a leadership update from this project context. Highlight current status, key decisions, risks, blockers, and requested actions: [paste context].

prompt_templates.title: Incident Communication Package
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when an incident needs multiple internal communication artifacts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft an internal incident report and a short FAQ from these details. Include impact, current status, owner, next update time, and open questions: [paste details].

output_examples.input: Notes about completed launch work, next sprint goals, and one unresolved dependency.
output_examples.output: A 3P update with sections for progress, plans, and problems, written in concise internal language.

output_examples.input: Announcements, hiring milestones, product updates, and reminders for the next month.
output_examples.output: A company newsletter draft with organized sections, clear headlines, and action items for employees.

output_examples.input: Incident timeline, affected users, mitigation status, and follow-up owner.
output_examples.output: An incident report that summarizes impact, response actions, current status, and next steps.

best_practices: Provide audience, purpose, deadline, and source notes before asking for a draft.
Review facts, names, dates, and ownership before sending any communication.
Ask for a shorter or more detailed version when the first draft misses the audience need.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to invent company facts or decisions.
Do not publish sensitive incident, HR, or legal content without internal approval.
Do not ask for a final message when the audience or communication type is unclear.

faq.question: What communication formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports 3P updates, newsletters, FAQ responses, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.

faq.question: Can it send messages for me?
faq.answer: No. It drafts content only. You must review, approve, and send the message through your normal tools.

faq.question: Does it know my company style guide?
faq.answer: It follows the guidance included in the skill. Add your style rules in the prompt for better alignment.

faq.question: Can it handle confidential information?
faq.answer: Use your organization policy for confidential content. Remove unnecessary secrets and review the output before sharing.

faq.question: What should I include in my prompt?
faq.answer: Include audience, goal, key facts, desired format, tone, deadline, and any required approvals or caveats.

faq.question: Why might it ask for clarification?
faq.answer: It asks for clarification when the requested communication type or required format is not clear.
